title: China's Geoeconomic Ascent and Global Trade Realignment
section_path: China's Geoeconomic Ascent and Global Trade Realignment
section_path_text: China's Geoeconomic Ascent and Global Trade Realignment
summary:
Main
Between 2000 and 2024, China transformed from a relatively marginal player in global trade into the world's leading trading hub, surpassing the United States as the top trade partner for more than 130 countries. One of the key catalysts for the U.S.-China trade war was the ballooning American trade deficit with China, which surged from under $50 billion in 1990 to nearly $540 billion by the mid-2020s, triggering strategic responses from Washington. The ripple effects of the trade war have been felt across multiple shipping sectors, though unevenly.
